   194→def parse_claude_transcript(path: Path) -> ParsedTranscript:
   195→    records = load_jsonl(path)
   196→    session_id = path.stem
   197→    title = path.stem
   198→    started_at = normalize_timestamp(path.stat().st_mtime)
   199→    cwd = ""
   200→    branch = ""
   201→    messages: list[TranscriptMessage] = []
   202→    tool_events: list[TranscriptToolEvent] = []
   203→
   204→    for record in records:
   205→        record_type = str(record.get("type") or "")
   206→        timestamp = normalize_timestamp(record.get("timestamp"), started_at)
   207→        session_id = str(record.get("sessionId") or session_id)
   208→        started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, record.get("timestamp"))
   209→        cwd = str(record.get("cwd") or cwd)
   210→        branch = str(record.get("gitBranch") or branch)
   211→
   212→        if record_type not in {"user", "assistant"}:
   213→            continue
   214→
   215→        message = ensure_dict(record.get("message"))
   216→        role = str(message.get("role") or record_type).lower()
   217→        text_parts: list[str] = []
   218→        content = message.get("content")
   219→        if isinstance(content, str):
   220→            text = content.strip()
   221→            if role in {"user", "assistant"} and text:
   222→                messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
   223→            continue
   224→
   225→        for block in ensure_list(content):
   226→            if isinstance(block, str):
   227→                if block.strip():
   228→                    text_parts.append(block.strip())
   229→                continue
   230→            if not isinstance(block, dict):
   231→                continue
   232→            block_type = str(block.get("type") or "").lower()
   233→            if block_type in TEXT_BLOCK_TYPES:
   234→                text = str(block.get("text") or "").strip()
   235→                if text:
   236→                    text_parts.append(text)
   237→                continue
   238→            if block_type in SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES:
   239→                continue
   240→            if block_type in TOOL_USE_TYPES:
   241→                name = str(block.get("name") or "tool_use")
   242→                tool_events.append(
   243→                    TranscriptToolEvent(
   244→                        summary=f"{name} input={summarize_value(block.get('input'))}",
   245→                        timestamp=timestamp,
   246→                    )
   247→                )
   248→                continue
   249→            if block_type in TOOL_RESULT_TYPES:
   250→                tool_use_id = str(block.get("tool_use_id") or "tool_result")
   251→                tool_events.append(
   252→                    TranscriptToolEvent(
   253→                        summary=f"{tool_use_id} output={summarize_value(block.get('content'))}",
   254→                        timestamp=timestamp,
   255→                    )
   256→                )
   257→
   258→        text = join_paragraphs(text_parts)
   259→        if role in {"user", "assistant"} and text:
   260→            messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
   261→
   262→    return ParsedTranscript(
   263→        client="claude",
   264→        session_id=session_id,
   265→        title=title,
   266→        started_at=started_at,
   267→        cwd=cwd,
   268→        branch=branch,
   269→        messages=messages,
   270→        tool_events=tool_events,
   271→        source_path=path,
   272→    )
   273→
   274→
   275→def parse_gemini_transcript(path: Path) -> ParsedTranscript:
   276→    session_id = path.stem
   277→    title = path.stem
   278→    started_at = normalize_timestamp(path.stat().st_mtime)
   279→    cwd = ""
   280→    branch = ""
   281→    messages: list[TranscriptMessage] = []
   282→    tool_events: list[TranscriptToolEvent] = []
   283→
   284→    if path.suffix.lower() == ".jsonl":
   285→        payload: Any = load_jsonl(path)
   286→    else:
   287→        payload = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   288→
   289→    message_signatures: set[tuple[str, str, str | None]] = set()
   290→    first_meta = find_first_mapping_with_keys(payload, {"sessionId", "chatId", "cwd", "branch", "timestamp"})
   291→    if first_meta:
   292→        session_id = str(first_meta.get("sessionId") or first_meta.get("chatId") or first_meta.get("id") or session_id)
   293→        started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, first_meta.get("timestamp"))
   294→        cwd = str(first_meta.get("cwd") or first_meta.get("projectRoot") or cwd)
   295→        branch = str(first_meta.get("branch") or branch)
   296→
   297→    def visit(node: Any) -> None:
   298→        nonlocal session_id, started_at, cwd, branch
   299→        if isinstance(node, list):
   300→            for item in node:
   301→                visit(item)
   302→            return
   303→        if not isinstance(node, dict):
   304→            return
   305→
   306→        session_id = str(node.get("sessionId") or node.get("chatId") or node.get("id") or session_id)
   307→        started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, node.get("timestamp"))
   308→        cwd = str(node.get("cwd") or node.get("projectRoot") or cwd)
   309→        branch = str(node.get("branch") or branch)
   310→
   311→        role = str(node.get("role") or node.get("author") or node.get("sender") or "").lower()
   312→        timestamp = normalize_timestamp(node.get("timestamp"), started_at)
   313→        if role in {"user", "assistant", "model"}:
   314→            normalized_role = "assistant" if role == "model" else role
   315→            text = extract_gemini_text(node)
   316→            signature = (normalized_role, text, timestamp)
   317→            if text and signature not in message_signatures:
   318→                message_signatures.add(signature)
   319→                messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=normalized_role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
   320→
   321→        tool_name = (
   322→            get_nested(node, "toolUse", "name")
   323→            or node.get("toolName")
   324→            or node.get("functionName")
   325→            or node.get("tool")
   326→        )
   327→        if tool_name:
   328→            tool_events.append(
   329→                TranscriptToolEvent(
   330→                    summary=f"{tool_name} input={summarize_value(node.get('args') or node.get('input'))}",
   331→                    timestamp=timestamp,
   332→                )
   333→            )
   334→
   335→        for value in node.values():
   336→            visit(value)
   337→
   338→    visit(payload)
   339→
   340→    return ParsedTranscript(
   341→        client="gemini",
   342→        session_id=session_id,
   343→        title=title,
   344→        started_at=started_at,
   345→        cwd=cwd,
   346→        branch=branch,
   347→        messages=messages,
   348→        tool_events=tool_events,
   349→        source_path=path,
   350→    )
   351→
   352→
   353→def import_transcript(
   354→    parsed: ParsedTranscript,
   355→    root: Path,
   356→    global_root: Path,
   357→    project_slug: str,
   358→    raw_upload_permission: str,
   359→    mirror_to_repo: bool = True,
   360→) -> dict[str, Any]:
   361→    imported_at = normalize_timestamp(datetime.now(timezone.utc))
   362→    raw_sha256 = sha256_file(parsed.source_path)
   363→    session_label = slugify(parsed.session_id, fallback=slugify(parsed.source_path.stem, fallback="session"))
   364→    artifact_stem = f"{session_label}__{raw_sha256[:8]}"
   365→    source_suffix = parsed.source_path.suffix or ".txt"
   366→
   367→    year_token, month_token = timestamp_partition(parsed.started_at or imported_at)
   368→
   369→    repo_root = root / "Memory" / "06_transcripts"
   370→    repo_raw_path = repo_root / "raw" / parsed.client / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}{source_suffix}"
   371→    repo_clean_path = repo_root / "clean" / parsed.client / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}.md"
   372→    repo_manifest_path = repo_root / "manifests" / parsed.client / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}.json"
   373→
   374→    global_raw_path = global_root / "raw" / parsed.client / project_slug / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}{source_suffix}"
   375→    global_clean_path = global_root / "clean" / parsed.client / project_slug / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}.md"
   376→    global_manifest_path = global_root / "index" / "manifests" / parsed.client / project_slug / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}.json"
   377→    global_event_log_path = global_root / "index" / "sessions.jsonl"
   378→    global_db_path = global_root / "index" / "search.sqlite"
   379→
   380→    for path in (
   381→        global_raw_path,
   382→        global_clean_path,
   383→        global_manifest_path,
   384→        global_event_log_path,
   385→        global_db_path,
   386→    ):
   387→        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   388→    if mirror_to_repo:
   389→        for path in (repo_raw_path, repo_clean_path, repo_manifest_path):
   390→            path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   391→
   392→    if mirror_to_repo:
   393→        copy_file(parsed.source_path, repo_raw_path)
   394→    copy_file(parsed.source_path, global_raw_path)
   395→
   396→    manifest = {
   397→        "client": parsed.client,
   398→        "project": project_slug,
   399→        "session_id": parsed.session_id,
   400→        "title": parsed.title,
   401→        "started_at": parsed.started_at,
   402→        "imported_at": imported_at,
   403→        "cwd": parsed.cwd,
   404→        "branch": parsed.branch,
   405→        "raw_source_path": parsed.source_path.as_posix(),
   406→        "raw_sha256": raw_sha256,
   407→        "raw_upload_permission": raw_upload_permission,
   408→        "repo_raw_path": repo_raw_path.relative_to(root).as_posix() if mirror_to_repo else "",
   409→        "repo_clean_path": repo_clean_path.relative_to(root).as_posix() if mirror_to_repo else "",
   410→        "repo_manifest_path": repo_manifest_path.relative_to(root).as_posix() if mirror_to_repo else "",
   411→        "global_raw_path": global_raw_path.as_posix(),
   412→        "global_clean_path": global_clean_path.as_posix(),
   413→        "global_manifest_path": global_manifest_path.as_posix(),
   414→        "message_count": len(parsed.messages),
   415→        "tool_event_count": len(parsed.tool_events),
   416→        "cleaning_mode": "deterministic-code",
   417→        "repo_mirror_enabled": mirror_to_repo,
   418→    }
   419→    existing_global_manifest = load_json(global_manifest_path)
   420→    manifest = preserve_existing_import_timestamp(existing_global_manifest, manifest)
   421→
   422→    if mirror_to_repo:
   423→        write_clean_markdown(parsed, manifest, repo_clean_path)
   424→    write_clean_markdown(parsed, manifest, global_clean_path)
   425→    if mirror_to_repo:
   426→        write_json(repo_manifest_path, manifest)
   427→    append_to_event_log = existing_global_manifest is None or existing_global_manifest != manifest
   428→    write_json(global_manifest_path, manifest)
   429→    if append_to_event_log:
   430→        append_jsonl(global_event_log_path, manifest)
   431→    upsert_search_index(global_db_path, manifest)
   432→
   433→    result = dict(manifest)
   434→    result["global_index_db"] = global_db_path.as_posix()
   435→    return result
   436→
   437→
   438→def write_clean_markdown(parsed: ParsedTranscript, manifest: dict[str, Any], path: Path) -> None:
   439→    lines = [
   440→        "---",
   441→        f"client: {manifest['client']}",
   442→        f"project: {manifest['project']}",
   443→        f"session_id: {manifest['session_id']}",
   444→        f"title: {yaml_escape(manifest['title'])}",
   445→        f"started_at: {manifest['started_at']}",
   446→        f"imported_at: {manifest['imported_at']}",
   447→        f"cwd: {yaml_escape(manifest['cwd'])}",
   448→        f"branch: {yaml_escape(manifest['branch'])}",
   449→        f"raw_source_path: {yaml_escape(manifest['raw_source_path'])}",
   450→        f"raw_sha256: {manifest['raw_sha256']}",
   451→        f"raw_upload_permission: {manifest['raw_upload_permission']}",
   452→        f"cleaning_mode: {manifest['cleaning_mode']}",
   453→        "---",
   454→        "",
   455→        f"# {manifest['title'] or manifest['session_id']}",
   456→        "",
   457→        "## Metadata",
   458→        f"- Client: `{manifest['client']}`",
   459→        f"- Project: `{manifest['project']}`",
   460→        f"- Session ID: `{manifest['session_id']}`",
   461→        f"- Started At: `{manifest['started_at']}`",
   462→        f"- Imported At: `{manifest['imported_at']}`",
   463→        f"- Raw SHA256: `{manifest['raw_sha256']}`",
   464→        f"- Raw Source: `{manifest['raw_source_path']}`",
   465→        f"- Repo Raw Path: `{manifest['repo_raw_path']}`",
   466→        f"- Repo Clean Path: `{manifest['repo_clean_path']}`",
   467→        "",
   468→        "## Messages",
   469→    ]
   470→
   471→    if parsed.messages:
   472→        for index, message in enumerate(parsed.messages, start=1):
   473→            lines.extend(
   474→                [
   475→                    f"### {index}. {message.role}",
   476→                    f"- Timestamp: `{message.timestamp or manifest['started_at']}`",
   477→                    "",
   478→                    message.text,
   479→                    "",
   480→                ]
   481→            )
   482→    else:
   483→        lines.extend(
   484→            [
   485→                "No user or assistant messages were extracted deterministically from the source transcript.",
   486→                "",
   487→            ]
   488→        )
   489→
   490→    lines.append("## Tool Events")
   491→    if parsed.tool_events:
   492→        for event in parsed.tool_events:
   493→            lines.append(f"- `{event.timestamp or manifest['started_at']}` {event.summary}")
   494→    else:
   495→        lines.append("- No tool events were extracted.")
   496→    lines.append("")
   497→
   498→    path.write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")
   499→

   501→def upsert_search_index(db_path: Path, manifest: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   502→    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
   503→    try:
   504→        conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
   505→        conn.execute(
   506→            """
   507→            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts (
   508→                client TEXT NOT NULL,
   509→                project TEXT NOT NULL,
   510→                session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
   511→                raw_sha256 TEXT NOT NULL,
   512→                title TEXT NOT NULL,
   513→                started_at TEXT NOT NULL,
   514→                imported_at TEXT NOT NULL,
   515→                cwd TEXT NOT NULL,
   516→                branch TEXT NOT NULL,
   517→                raw_source_path TEXT NOT NULL,
   518→                raw_upload_permission TEXT NOT NULL,
   519→                global_raw_path TEXT NOT NULL,
   520→                global_clean_path TEXT NOT NULL,
   521→                repo_raw_path TEXT NOT NULL,
   522→                repo_clean_path TEXT NOT NULL,
   523→                repo_manifest_path TEXT NOT NULL,
   524→                message_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
   525→                tool_event_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
   526→                PRIMARY KEY (client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)
   527→            )
   528→            """
   529→        )
   530→        conn.execute(
   531→            """
   532→            INSERT INTO transcripts (
   533→                client,
   534→                project,
   535→                session_id,
   536→                raw_sha256,
   537→                title,
   538→                started_at,
   539→                imported_at,
   540→                cwd,
   541→                branch,
   542→                raw_source_path,
   543→                raw_upload_permission,
   544→                global_raw_path,
   545→                global_clean_path,
   546→                repo_raw_path,
   547→                repo_clean_path,
   548→                repo_manifest_path,
   549→                message_count,
   550→                tool_event_count
   551→            ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   552→            ON CONFLICT(client, project, session_id, raw_sha256) DO UPDATE SET
   553→                title = excluded.title,
   554→                started_at = excluded.started_at,
   555→                imported_at = excluded.imported_at,
   556→                cwd = excluded.cwd,
   557→                branch = excluded.branch,
   558→                raw_source_path = excluded.raw_source_path,
   559→                raw_upload_permission = excluded.raw_upload_permission,
   560→                global_raw_path = excluded.global_raw_path,
   561→                global_clean_path = excluded.global_clean_path,
   562→                repo_raw_path = excluded.repo_raw_path,
   563→                repo_clean_path = excluded.repo_clean_path,
   564→                repo_manifest_path = excluded.repo_manifest_path,
   565→                message_count = excluded.message_count,
   566→                tool_event_count = excluded.tool_event_count
   567→            """,
   568→            (
   569→                manifest["client"],
   570→                manifest["project"],
   571→                manifest["session_id"],
   572→                manifest["raw_sha256"],
   573→                manifest["title"],
   574→                manifest["started_at"],
   575→                manifest["imported_at"],
   576→                manifest["cwd"],
   577→                manifest["branch"],
   578→                manifest["raw_source_path"],
   579→                manifest["raw_upload_permission"],
   580→                manifest["global_raw_path"],
   581→                manifest["global_clean_path"],
   582→                manifest["repo_raw_path"],
   583→                manifest["repo_clean_path"],
   584→                manifest["repo_manifest_path"],
   585→                manifest["message_count"],
   586→                manifest["tool_event_count"],
   587→            ),
   588→        )
   589→        conn.commit()
   590→    finally:
   591→        conn.close()

   594→def copy_file(source: Path, destination: Path) -> None:
   595→    if source.resolve() == destination.resolve():
   596→        return
   597→    shutil.copy2(source, destination)
   598→
   599→
   600→def transcript_has_content(parsed: ParsedTranscript) -> bool:
   601→    return bool(parsed.messages or parsed.tool_events)
   602→
   603→
   604→def write_json(path: Path, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   605→    path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
   606→
   607→
   608→def manifest_changed(path: Path, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   609→    if not path.exists():
   610→        return True
   611→    try:
   612→        current = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   613→    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
   614→        return True
   615→    return current != payload
   616→
   617→
   618→def preserve_existing_import_timestamp(existing: dict[str, Any] | None, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
   619→    if existing is None:
   620→        return payload
   621→    if manifest_signature(existing) != manifest_signature(payload):
   622→        return payload
   623→    preserved = dict(payload)
   624→    imported_at = existing.get("imported_at")
   625→    if isinstance(imported_at, str) and imported_at:
   626→        preserved["imported_at"] = imported_at
   627→    return preserved
   628→
   629→
   630→def manifest_signature(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
   631→    return {key: value for key, value in payload.items() if key != "imported_at"}
   632→
   633→
   634→def append_jsonl(path: Path, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   635→    with path.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
   636→        handle.write(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
   637→
   638→
   639→def load_jsonl(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   640→    records: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   641→    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as handle:
   642→        for line in handle:
   643→            line = line.strip()
   644→            if not line:
   645→                continue
   646→            try:
   647→                records.append(json.loads(line))
   648→            except json.JSONDecodeError:
   649→                continue
   650→    return records
   651→
   652→
   653→def load_json(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
   654→    if not path.exists():
   655→        return None
   656→    try:
   657→        return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   658→    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
   659→        return None
   660→
   661→
   662→def ensure_dict(value: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
   663→    return value if isinstance(value, dict) else {}
   664→
   665→
   666→def ensure_list(value: Any) -> list[Any]:
   667→    return value if isinstance(value, list) else []
   668→
   669→
   670→def get_nested(payload: dict[str, Any], *keys: str) -> Any:
   671→    current: Any = payload
   672→    for key in keys:
   673→        if not isinstance(current, dict):
   674→            return None
   675→        current = current.get(key)
   676→    return current
   677→
   678→
   679→def extract_text_blocks(blocks: Any) -> str:
   680→    parts: list[str] = []
   681→    for block in ensure_list(blocks):
   682→        if isinstance(block, str):
   683→            text = block.strip()
   684→            if text:
   685→                parts.append(text)
   686→            continue
   687→        if not isinstance(block, dict):
   688→            continue
   689→        block_type = str(block.get("type") or "").lower()
   690→        if block_type in SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES | TOOL_USE_TYPES | TOOL_RESULT_TYPES:
   691→            continue
   692→        if block_type in TEXT_BLOCK_TYPES:
   693→            text = str(block.get("text") or "").strip()
   694→            if text:
   695→                parts.append(text)
   696→            continue
   697→        fallback_text = extract_simple_text(block)
   698→        if fallback_text:
   699→            parts.append(fallback_text)
   700→    return join_paragraphs(parts)
   701→
   702→
   703→def extract_simple_text(value: Any) -> str:
   704→    if isinstance(value, str):
   705→        return value.strip()
   706→    if isinstance(value, list):
   707→        return join_paragraphs(extract_simple_text(item) for item in value)
   708→    if isinstance(value, dict):
   709→        for key in ("text", "content", "value"):
   710→            if key in value and isinstance(value[key], str):
   711→                return value[key].strip()
   712→        return ""
   713→    return ""
   714→
   715→
   716→def extract_gemini_text(node: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
   717→    parts: list[str] = []
   718→    for candidate in (node.get("parts"), node.get("content"), node.get("text"), node.get("message")):
   719→        parts.extend(extract_gemini_parts(candidate))
   720→    return join_paragraphs(parts)
   721→
   722→
   723→def extract_gemini_parts(value: Any) -> list[str]:
   724→    if isinstance(value, str):
   725→        text = value.strip()
   726→        return [text] if text else []
   727→    if isinstance(value, list):
   728→        parts: list[str] = []
   729→        for item in value:
   730→            parts.extend(extract_gemini_parts(item))
   731→        return parts
   732→    if isinstance(value, dict):
   733→        block_type = str(value.get("type") or "").lower()
   734→        if block_type in SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES | TOOL_USE_TYPES | TOOL_RESULT_TYPES:
   735→            return []
   736→        if "text" in value and isinstance(value["text"], str):
   737→            text = value["text"].strip()
   738→            return [text] if text else []
   739→        parts: list[str] = []
   740→        for key in ("parts", "content"):
   741→            if key in value:
   742→                parts.extend(extract_gemini_parts(value[key]))
   743→        return parts
   744→    return []
   745→
   746→
   747→def find_first_mapping_with_keys(value: Any, keys: set[str]) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
   748→    if isinstance(value, dict):
   749→        if keys & set(value.keys()):
   750→            return value
   751→        for candidate in value.values():
   752→            result = find_first_mapping_with_keys(candidate, keys)
   753→            if result is not None:
   754→                return result
   755→        return None
   756→    if isinstance(value, list):
   757→        for candidate in value:
   758→            result = find_first_mapping_with_keys(candidate, keys)
   759→            if result is not None:
   760→                return result
   761→    return None
   762→
   763→
   764→def normalize_timestamp(value: Any, fallback: str | None = None) -> str:
   765→    if isinstance(value, (int, float)):
   766→        return (
   767→            datetime.fromtimestamp(float(value), tz=timezone.utc)
   768→            .replace(microsecond=0)
   769→            .isoformat()
   770→            .replace("+00:00", "Z")
   771→        )
   772→    if isinstance(value, datetime):
   773→        normalized = value.astimezone(timezone.utc) if value.tzinfo else value
   774→        return normalized.replace(microsecond=0).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z")
   775→    if isinstance(value, str):
   776→        text = value.strip()
   777→        if not text:
   778→            return fallback or normalize_timestamp(datetime.now(timezone.utc))
   779→        iso_candidate = text[:-1] + "+00:00" if text.endswith("Z") else text
   780→        try:
   781→            parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(iso_candidate)
   782→            if parsed.tzinfo is not None:
   783→                parsed = parsed.astimezone(timezone.utc)
   784→            return parsed.replace(microsecond=0).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z")
   785→        except ValueError:
   786→            return text
   787→    return fallback or normalize_timestamp(datetime.now(timezone.utc))
   788→
   789→
   790→def earliest_timestamp(current: str, candidate: Any) -> str:
   791→    normalized = normalize_timestamp(candidate, current)
   792→    current_dt = parse_iso_timestamp(current)
   793→    normalized_dt = parse_iso_timestamp(normalized)
   794→    if current_dt is not None and normalized_dt is not None:
   795→        return normalized if normalized_dt < current_dt else current
   796→    if normalized_dt is not None:
   797→        return normalized
   798→    return current or normalized
   799→
   800→
   801→def parse_iso_timestamp(value: str) -> datetime | None:
   802→    if not value:
   803→        return None
   804→    candidate = value[:-1] + "+00:00" if value.endswith("Z") else value
   805→    try:
   806→        parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(candidate)
   807→    except ValueError:
   808→        return None
   809→    if parsed.tzinfo is None:
   810→        return parsed.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
   811→    return parsed.astimezone(timezone.utc)
   812→
   813→
   814→def timestamp_partition(timestamp: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
   815→    try:
   816→        candidate = timestamp[:-1] + "+00:00" if timestamp.endswith("Z") else timestamp
   817→        parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(candidate)
   818→        return parsed.strftime("%Y"), parsed.strftime("%m")
   819→    except ValueError:
   820→        return "unknown", "unknown"
   821→
   822→
   823→def join_paragraphs(parts: Any) -> str:
   824→    cleaned = [str(part).strip() for part in parts if str(part).strip()]
   825→    return "\n\n".join(cleaned)
   826→
   827→
   828→def summarize_value(value: Any, limit: int = 180) -> str:
   829→    if value is None:
   830→        return "none"
   831→    if isinstance(value, str):
   832→        text = " ".join(value.split())
   833→        return truncate(text, limit)
   834→    try:
   835→        text = json.dumps(value, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True)
   836→    except TypeError:
   837→        text = str(value)
   838→    text = " ".join(text.split())
   839→    return truncate(text, limit)
   840→
   841→
   842→def truncate(text: str, limit: int) -> str:
   843→    return text if len(text) <= limit else text[: limit - 3] + "..."
   844→
   845→
   846→def yaml_escape(value: str) -> str:
   847→    escaped = value.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace('"', '\\"').replace("\n", "\\n")
   848→    return f'"{escaped}"'
   849→
   850→
   851→def discover_source_files(clients: set[str] | None = None) -> list[tuple[str, Path]]:
   852→    requested = clients or CLIENTS
   853→    roots = default_client_roots()
   854→    patterns = {
   855→        "codex": ["sessions/**/*.jsonl"],
   856→        "claude": ["projects/**/*.jsonl"],
   857→        "gemini": [
   858→            "tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.json",
   859→            "tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.jsonl",
   860→            "history/**/*.json",
   861→            "history/**/*.jsonl",
   862→            "chats/**/*.json",
   863→            "chats/**/*.jsonl",
   864→        ],
   865→    }
   866→    matches: list[tuple[str, Path]] = []
   867→    seen: set[str] = set()
   868→    for client in sorted(requested):
   869→        root = roots.get(client)
   870→        if root is None or not root.exists():
   871→            continue
   872→        for pattern in patterns[client]:
   873→            for path in root.glob(pattern):
   874→                if not path.is_file():
   875→                    continue
   876→                key = path.resolve().as_posix().lower()
   877→                if key in seen:
   878→                    continue
   879→                seen.add(key)
   880→                matches.append((client, path.resolve()))
   881→    matches.sort(
   882→        key=lambda item: (safe_file_mtime(item[1]), item[1].as_posix().lower()),
   883→        reverse=True,
   884→    )
   885→    return matches
   886→
   887→
   888→def infer_project_slug(parsed: ParsedTranscript) -> str:
   889→    if parsed.cwd:
   890→        try:
   891→            cwd_path = Path(parsed.cwd)
   892→            return slugify(cwd_path.name, fallback="project")
   893→        except OSError:
   894→            pass
   895→    if parsed.client == "claude":
   896→        parent_name = parsed.source_path.parent.name
   897→        parent_name = re.sub(r"^[a-z]--", "", parent_name.lower())
   898→        return slugify(parent_name, fallback="project")
   899→    if parsed.client == "gemini" and parsed.source_path.parent.name:
   900→        return slugify(parsed.source_path.parent.name, fallback="project")
   901→    return "unknown-project"
   902→
   903→
   904→def transcript_matches_repo(parsed: ParsedTranscript, repo_root: Path, repo_slug: str) -> bool:
   905→    repo_root = repo_root.resolve()
   906→    if parsed.cwd:
   907→        try:
   908→            cwd_path = Path(parsed.cwd).resolve()
   909→            if cwd_path == repo_root:
   910→                return True
   911→            if repo_root in cwd_path.parents:
   912→                return True
   913→        except OSError:
   914→            pass
   915→    return project_slugs_match(infer_project_slug(parsed), repo_slug)
   916→
   917→
   918→def project_slugs_match(left: str, right: str) -> bool:
   919→    return bool(left and right and left == right and left != "unknown-project")
   920→
   921→
   922→def safe_file_mtime(path: Path) -> float:
   923→    try:
   924→        return path.stat().st_mtime
   925→    except OSError:
   926→        return 0.0
   927→

1→# Git Policy
     2→
     3→MemoryTree must adapt to the host repository. It does not get to override it.
     4→
     5→## Safe Defaults
     6→
     7→- Do not mix MemoryTree-owned changes with product code changes by default.
     8→- Do not stage or push unrelated user work.
     9→- Do not assume direct commits to protected branches are allowed.
    10→- Do not bypass review, CI, E2E, or release controls.
    11→- Keep raw transcript files in the current project's transcript mirror by default, but exclude them from automatic staging unless the user explicitly approved raw transcript uploads for the current repository.
    12→- Do not mirror or stage transcript files from unrelated projects inside the current repository.
    13→
    14→## Policy Merge
    15→
    16→When the repo already has branch or PR rules:
    17→
    18→1. Keep the repo's stricter rule.
    19→2. Treat MemoryTree as documentation and process support.
    20→3. Only add MemoryTree-specific guidance that does not weaken repo controls.
    21→
    22→## MemoryTree-Owned Files
    23→
    24→Default whitelist:
    25→
    26→- `Memory/**`, except `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until the user explicitly approved raw transcript uploads for this repository
    27→- `AGENTS.md` only when the file is managed by MemoryTree or the user approved the merge
    28→
    29→If the diff includes files outside this whitelist, stop and ask before staging or pushing.
    30→
    31→## Default MemoryTree Flow
    32→
    33→Use this flow only for MemoryTree-only changes and only when the host repo does not forbid it:
    34→
    35→1. Stage only the MemoryTree-owned files created or updated by the skill.
    36→2. Create or reuse a dedicated branch such as `memorytree/<scope>-<yyyymmdd>-<slug>`.
    37→3. Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, but if the host repo enforces a commit convention, use a compatible equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
    38→4. Push that dedicated branch if the repository allows the current actor to push.
    39→5. Open a dedicated PR instead of merging directly into a protected branch.
    40→6. Enable auto-merge only when the repository still enforces its required approvals and checks.
    41→7. If transcript files are included, keep the PR scoped to one repository's transcript mirror only. Do not mix cross-project transcript archives into the same PR.
    42→8. Keep the global transcript archive updated in the same import operation, but never stage or push that user-level global archive as part of a repo PR.
    43→
    44→## Commit Guidance
    45→
    46→- Keep the title concise and specific.
    47→- Prefer scopes such as `agents`, `goals`, `todos`, `chat-logs`, `transcripts`, `knowledge`, `archive`, `policy`, or `bootstrap`.
    48→- If a body is needed, keep it lightweight: reason, affected files, and whether the change is MemoryTree-only.
    49→- If the host repo requires Conventional Commits or another enforced prefix, keep `memorytree` visible as the scope or memory tag rather than bypassing the repo rule.
    50→
    51→Example title:
    52→
    53→```text
    54→memorytree(todos): sync active todo progress
    55→```
    56→
    57→Host-compatible example:
    58→
    59→```text
    60→docs(memorytree): sync active todo progress
    61→```
    62→
    63→Example body:
    64→
    65→```text
    66→Reason:
    67→- align the active todo with confirmed progress
    68→
    69→Files:
    70→- Memory/02_todos/todo_v001_003_20260314.md
    71→- Memory/03_chat_logs/2026-03-14_09-30.md
    72→
    73→Notes:
    74→- memorytree-only change
    75→- no product code included
    76→```
    77→
    78→## PR Guidance
    79→
    80→- Reuse the commit title as the PR title when it still describes the change accurately.
    81→- Keep the PR scoped to MemoryTree-owned files only.
    82→- State whether the PR is MemoryTree-only and whether auto-merge was enabled.
    83→- State whether raw transcripts were included or intentionally left unstaged in the repo mirror.
    84→- Follow the repository's required target branch, review policy, and CI gates.
    85→
    86→## Stop And Ask
    87→
    88→Stop and ask the user before committing, pushing, or opening a PR when any of these are true:
    89→
    90→1. The diff touches product code or files outside the MemoryTree whitelist.
    91→2. `AGENTS.md` has user-managed policy content that cannot be safely merged automatically.
    92→3. The target branch, base branch, or PR destination is unclear.
    93→4. The repository forbids bot pushes, automated PRs, or auto-merge.
    94→5. The repository's review or CI rules conflict with the default MemoryTree-only flow.
    95→6. Raw transcript upload permission for the repository is unknown.
    96→7. The diff includes raw transcripts or cleaned transcript indexes from other projects.
    97→

1→# Project Detection
     2→
     3→Use this file before changing any MemoryTree files.
     4→
     5→## States
     6→
     7→### `not-installed`
     8→
     9→Treat the repo as `not-installed` when any of these are true:
    10→
    11→- No `Memory/` directory exists.
    12→- No `AGENTS.md` exists and there are no active goal, bound todo, or chat-log files to preserve.
    13→
    14→Action:
    15→
    16→1. Run `scripts/init-memorytree.py`.
    17→2. Review the generated files.
    18→3. Use fresh init only when there is no `AGENTS.md` or other repo policy source to preserve. If repo policy already exists, switch to `scripts/upgrade-memorytree.py`.
    19→
    20→### `partial`
    21→
    22→Treat the repo as `partial` when MemoryTree exists but one or more expected pieces are missing:
    23→
    24→- `Memory/` exists but one of `01_goals`, `02_todos`, or `03_chat_logs` is missing.
    25→- `AGENTS.md` already exists even if `Memory/` does not yet exist. Use upgrade to avoid overwriting repo policy.
    26→- Common repo policy sources already exist, such as policy-bearing `CONTRIBUTING.md`, PR templates, `CODEOWNERS`, or commitlint config. Use upgrade to avoid conflicting Git guidance.
    27→- Goal files exist but there is no bound todo for the latest goal.
    28→- A chat-log directory exists but no current chat-log file exists.
    29→- `AGENTS.md` is missing or does not mention the MemoryTree read order, isolated commit/PR flow, and safety rules.
    30→
    31→Action:
    32→
    33→1. Run `scripts/upgrade-memorytree.py`.
    34→2. Preserve stricter repo policies already present in `AGENTS.md`.
    35→
    36→### `installed`
    37→
    38→Treat the repo as `installed` when these are all true:
    39→
    40→- `Memory/01_goals/`, `Memory/02_todos/`, and `Memory/03_chat_logs/` exist.
    41→- At least one goal file exists.
    42→- At least one todo file bound to the latest goal exists.
    43→- At least one current chat-log file exists.
    44→- `AGENTS.md` exists and already carries MemoryTree read-order, isolated commit/PR, and safety guidance.
    45→
    46→Action:
    47→
    48→1. Read active files in order.
    49→2. Update only the active goal, active todo, and current session log as needed.
    50→
    51→## Detection Notes
    52→
    53→- Do not require `04_knowledge` or `05_archive` for a valid install.
    54→- Do not require `06_transcripts/` for a valid install. Transcript archival is optional; when enabled, keep the current-project mirror and the user-level global archive synchronized together.
    55→- Fresh init now checks common non-`AGENTS.md` policy files such as PR templates, `CODEOWNERS`, commitlint config, and policy-bearing `CONTRIBUTING.md`. Generic contributor docs without Git or PR rules do not block fresh init by themselves.
    56→- Do not replace an existing `AGENTS.md` blindly. Merge carefully.
    57→- If the repo already has its own memory system, ask before adding a second one.
    58→

1→# Memory Layout
     2→
     3→Use this layout unless the host repo already has an equivalent structure.
     4→
     5→```text
     6→Memory/
     7→  01_goals/
     8→  02_todos/
     9→  03_chat_logs/
    10→  04_knowledge/
    11→  05_archive/
    12→  06_transcripts/
    13→    clean/
    14→    manifests/
    15→    raw/
    16→```
    17→
    18→## Naming
    19→
    20→- Goal: `goal_vNNN_YYYYMMDD.md`
    21→- Todo: `todo_vNNN_SSS_YYYYMMDD.md`
    22→- Chat log: `YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`
    23→
    24→Where:
    25→
    26→- `NNN` is the zero-padded goal version.
    27→- `SSS` is the zero-padded todo subversion bound to one goal version.
    28→
    29→## Required Files
    30→
    31→- One active goal file in `01_goals/`.
    32→- One active todo file bound to the latest goal in `02_todos/`.
    33→- One current chat log in `03_chat_logs/`.
    34→
    35→## Optional Files
    36→
    37→- `04_knowledge/` for durable project notes, specs, and decisions.
    38→- `05_archive/` for retired goals, todos, and old logs.
    39→- `06_transcripts/clean/` for project-facing cleaned transcript indexes grouped by client.
    40→- `06_transcripts/manifests/` for per-session metadata such as raw source path, hash, import time, and client.
    41→- `06_transcripts/raw/` for project-local raw transcript mirrors grouped by client. Keep these files in the repo even when raw transcript uploads are not approved; approval controls Git staging and push, not local storage.
    42→
    43→## Transcript Notes
    44→
    45→- Keep a synchronized global transcript archive outside the repository in a user-level MemoryTree store.
    46→- Organize transcript imports by `client / project / session`, both in the current repository and in the global archive.
    47→- Keep the repository mirror limited to the current project's transcripts. Cross-project backfills belong in the global archive only.
    48→- Use deterministic code to generate `clean/` files from `raw/` files. Do not rely on model output for transcript normalization.
    49→- Supported client families are `codex`, `claude`, and `gemini`.
    50→

1→# Locale Selection
     2→
     3→Use this file when initializing MemoryTree for a repo that did not specify a language.
     4→
     5→## Rule Order
     6→
     7→1. If the user explicitly provides `--locale`, use it.
     8→2. Otherwise use `--locale auto`.
     9→3. In `auto` mode, inspect the repository first.
    10→4. If the repository language is unclear, fall back to the system locale.
    11→5. If both are unclear, fall back to `en`.
    12→
    13→## Repo Inspection
    14→
    15→Prefer these files and locations:
    16→
    17→- `AGENTS.md`
    18→- `README.md`
    19→- `README.zh-CN.md`
    20→- `README.zh_CN.md`
    21→- top-level Markdown or text files
    22→- `docs/**/*.md`
    23→
    24→If the inspected content is mostly Chinese, choose `zh-cn`.
    25→If it is mostly English or no Chinese signal exists, choose `en`.
    26→Treat short Latin-only repo text, such as a small English `README.md`, as an English signal.
    27→If the repository is mixed-language and neither side clearly dominates, treat the repo language as unclear and fall back to the system locale.
    28→
    29→## Supported Locales
    30→
    31→- `en`
    32→- `zh-cn`
    33→
    34→## Aliases
    35→
    36→Map these aliases to `en`:
    37→
    38→- `en-us`
    39→- `en-gb`
    40→
    41→Map these aliases to `zh-cn`:
    42→
    43→- `zh`
    44→- `zh-cn`
    45→- `zh-sg`
    46→- `zh-hans`
    47→- `zh-hant`
    48→- `zh-tw`
    49→- `zh-hk`
    50→
    51→Current behavior intentionally collapses all Chinese variants to `zh-cn` because the skill currently ships Simplified Chinese templates only.
    52→

1→# Update Rules
     2→
     3→Use these rules after the repo is already initialized.
     4→
     5→## Goal Updates
     6→
     7→Create a new goal version only when the user confirms a real requirement or scope change, such as:
     8→
     9→- The long-term objective changed.
    10→- Acceptance criteria changed.
    11→- The project focus moved to a different bounded problem.
    12→
    13→Do not create a new goal version for routine execution progress.
    14→
    15→## Todo Updates
    16→
    17→Update the active todo when:
    18→
    19→- Progress changed.
    20→- The next execution step changed.
    21→- A milestone moved from pending to active or done.
    22→- A new task is needed to satisfy the same goal.
    23→
    24→Create a new todo subversion when the goal version changes or the todo structure needs a clean reset.
    25→
    26→## Chat Log Updates
    27→
    28→- Append only.
    29→- Create a new chat file when the session boundary is explicit.
    30→- If the boundary is unclear, continue the active chat file unless the repo already defines a stricter rule.
    31→- Do not replace the active project chat log with imported raw transcripts. Use transcript imports as supporting evidence and search material.
    32→
    33→## Knowledge Updates
    34→
    35→Move stable, reusable information into `04_knowledge/`, for example:
    36→
    37→- Architecture decisions
    38→- External constraints
    39→- Reusable operating notes
    40→- Domain facts that should outlive a single session
    41→
    42→## Archive Updates
    43→
    44→Move retired or noisy context into `05_archive/` when it no longer belongs in the active files.
    45→
    46→## Transcript Import Updates
    47→
    48→- Preserve raw transcript files unchanged in both the current project's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/<client>/` mirror and the global archive.
    49→- Generate cleaned Markdown transcript indexes for discovery and lightweight review using deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
    50→- Import one transcript by updating the global archive in every case, and the current project's transcript mirror only when the source belongs to that repository.
    51→- Use `scripts/discover-transcripts.py` when the skill needs to scan local client stores instead of importing one explicit source path.
    52→- During discovery, mirror only transcripts that match the current repository into `Memory/06_transcripts/**`; import unrelated projects into the global archive only.
    53→- Skip snapshot-only or otherwise content-empty transcript files instead of generating empty clean transcripts.
    54→- Keep cleaned transcript indexes in `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/<client>/` and the matching global clean archive synchronized.
    55→- Raw transcript upload approval only controls Git staging and push for `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**`. It does not block project-local storage.
    56→- When a cleaned transcript result affects an important decision, verify the exact wording against the raw transcript before treating it as evidence.
    57→

1→# Response Language
     2→
     3→Use this file after locale detection. It governs how the assistant should write replies while using MemoryTree.
     4→
     5→## Rule Order
     6→
     7→1. If the user explicitly asks for a reply language, obey it.
     8→2. Otherwise prefer the user's current message language.
     9→3. If the message language is unclear, prefer the repository locale.
    10→4. If the repository locale is unclear, prefer the active MemoryTree template locale.
    11→5. If nothing is clear, default to `en`.
    12→
    13→## Important Distinction
    14→
    15→- Initialization locale controls which templates are written.
    16→- Response language controls how the assistant talks during setup and maintenance.
    17→
    18→These usually match, but they do not have to.
    19→
    20→Example:
    21→
    22→- A repo may use English templates.
    23→- The current user may still prefer Chinese discussion.
    24→
    25→In that case:
    26→
    27→- Keep template generation in English.
    28→- Reply in Chinese unless the user asks to switch.
    29→
    30→## Safety Rules
    31→
    32→- Do not rewrite existing MemoryTree files only to translate them.
    33→- Do not change file locale without explicit user confirmation.
    34→- When the repo is mixed-language, keep the existing file language stable and mirror the user's discussion language in replies.
    35→

1→# Transcript Archive
     2→
     3→Use this file when the user wants MemoryTree to preserve local AI chat history from Codex, Claude Code, or Gemini CLI.
     4→
     5→## Goal
     6→
     7→Keep a trustworthy transcript record by preserving the current project's transcript mirror and a synchronized global archive at the same time, without mixing unrelated projects into the current repository.
     8→
     9→## Core Rules
    10→
    11→1. Preserve raw transcripts unchanged as the source of truth.
    12→2. Generate cleaned Markdown transcript indexes for discovery, lightweight review, and cross-project search.
    13→3. Keep both the current repository mirror and the user-level global archive updated on every transcript import.
    14→4. Keep the repository mirror scoped to the current project only. When batch discovery sees other projects, archive them in the global archive only.
    15→5. If current-project ownership is ambiguous, treat the transcript as global-only until stronger evidence exists.
    16→6. Ask once per repository whether raw transcript files may also be committed and pushed with MemoryTree.
    17→7. Record that repository-specific answer in `AGENTS.md`.
    18→8. If the answer is not an explicit yes, keep raw transcripts in the repository mirror but exclude them from automatic staging and push.
    19→9. Use deterministic code for transcript cleaning. Do not spend model tokens to normalize transcript content when a parser script can do it.
    20→
    21→## Import Script
    22→
    23→Use `scripts/import-transcripts.py` for one known source file.
    24→
    25→The script must:
    26→
    27→1. Copy the raw transcript into the current repository mirror only when the source belongs to the current repository.
    28→2. Copy the raw transcript into the global archive.
    29→3. Generate the cleaned Markdown transcript in both places with deterministic code when the source matches the current repository, or in the global archive only when it does not.
    30→4. Write manifests and update the global search index in the same operation.
    31→5. Use a concurrency-safe global index, such as SQLite WAL mode, so multiple projects can update the global archive without corrupting it.
    32→6. Refuse snapshot-only or otherwise content-empty sources instead of archiving empty transcript shells.
    33→7. Treat missing or fuzzy project signals as not matched; do not mirror them into the repository on name similarity alone.
    34→
    35→## Discovery Script
    36→
    37→Use `scripts/discover-transcripts.py` when MemoryTree should scan the supported local client stores automatically.
    38→
    39→The script must:
    40→
    41→1. Discover supported transcript files from local Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini stores.
    42→2. Mirror transcripts that belong to the current repository into both the repository and the global archive.
    43→3. Backfill transcripts from unrelated projects into the global archive only.
    44→4. Skip unreadable or malformed transcript files without aborting the full discovery run.
    45→5. Skip snapshot-only or otherwise content-empty files instead of generating empty clean transcripts.
    46→6. Keep deterministic cleaning, manifest generation, and global index updates in the same operation.
    47→7. Support a current-project-only mode when the user wants to avoid global backfill during that run.
    48→8. Keep ambiguous ownership cases out of the repository mirror unless path-level evidence confirms they belong to the current repo.
    49→
    50→## Global Archive Layout
    51→
    52→Recommended user-level layout:
    53→
    54→```text
    55→~/.memorytree/transcripts/
    56→  raw/
    57→    codex/<project>/<yyyy>/<mm>/<session-id>.jsonl
    58→    claude/<project>/<yyyy>/<mm>/<session-id>.jsonl
    59→    gemini/<project>/<yyyy>/<mm>/<session-id>.json
    60→  clean/
    61→    codex/<project>/<yyyy>/<mm>/<session-id>.md
    62→    claude/<project>/<yyyy>/<mm>/<session-id>.md
    63→    gemini/<project>/<yyyy>/<mm>/<session-id>.md
    64→  index/
    65→    sessions.jsonl
    66→    search.sqlite
    67→```
    68→
    69→Path roots:
    70→
    71→- Windows: `%USERPROFILE%`
    72→- macOS and Ubuntu: `$HOME`
    73→
    74→## Project Mirror Layout
    75→
    76→Inside a repository, keep the current project's full mirror:
    77→
    78→```text
    79→Memory/06_transcripts/
    80→  clean/<client>/
    81→  manifests/
    82→  raw/<client>/
    83→```
    84→
    85→Do not place other projects' transcript mirrors inside the current repository.
    86→
    87→## Client Sources
    88→
    89→### Codex
    90→
    91→- Prefer full session files from `~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl`.
    92→- Use `~/.codex/session_index.jsonl` for session discovery when helpful.
    93→- Treat `~/.codex/history.jsonl` as lightweight history, not the preferred full transcript source.
    94→
    95→### Claude Code
    96→
    97→- Prefer `transcript_path` when the client exposes it.
    98→- Otherwise prefer `~/.claude/projects/<project>/*.jsonl`.
    99→- Treat `~/.claude/history.jsonl` as lightweight history, not the preferred full transcript source.
   100→- Remember that local transcript retention may be limited by the client's cleanup policy.
   101→
   102→### Gemini CLI
   103→
   104→- Gemini transcript import is supported, but availability depends on saved chats or enabled checkpointing.
   105→- Prefer saved chat or resume-friendly sources first.
   106→- If checkpointing is enabled, use the checkpoint history under `~/.gemini/tmp/<project_hash>/checkpoints` and related Git snapshots under `~/.gemini/history/<project_hash>`.
   107→- If no saved chat or checkpoint source exists, report that no importable Gemini transcript is currently available.
   108→
   109→## Import Rules
   110→
   111→1. Store the raw source unchanged in the global archive, and in the current repository only when the transcript belongs to that repository.
   112→2. Compute and record a content hash for the raw file.
   113→3. Generate a cleaned Markdown file with deterministic code, not model-generated paraphrasing.
   114→4. During batch discovery, mirror only current-project transcripts into the repository. Unrelated projects go to the global archive only.
   115→5. Each cleaned Markdown file must include:
   116→   - client
   117→   - project
   118→   - session id
   119→   - start time
   120→   - cwd or repo path when available
   121→   - branch when available
   122→   - raw source path
   123→   - raw content hash
   124→6. Keep user messages, assistant messages, and useful tool summaries in the cleaned transcript.
   125→7. Drop or fold noisy events such as queue operations, repeated metadata, telemetry, and large low-signal tool dumps.
   126→8. Never treat the cleaned transcript as stronger evidence than the raw transcript.
   127→
   128→## Search Rules
   129→
   130→When the user asks to search all memories or all chat history:
   131→
   132→1. Search the global cleaned transcript index first.
   133→2. Use the matching raw transcript files for exact confirmation.
   134→3. Then combine those results with the active repository's `Memory/` files when answering.
   135→
   136→## Git Rules
   137→
   138→1. Cleaned transcript indexes may be committed as MemoryTree-owned documentation for the current repository.
   139→2. Raw transcript files stay in the current repository mirror regardless of upload approval.
   140→3. Raw transcript files may be staged, committed, and pushed only after the user explicitly approved raw transcript uploads for that repository.
   141→4. Even when raw transcript uploads are approved, keep the PR limited to the current repository's transcript mirror. Do not mix transcript files from unrelated projects.
   142→

1→# Execution Environment
     2→
     3→Use this file when MemoryTree needs to run a bundled Python script inside an arbitrary repository.
     4→
     5→## Goal
     6→
     7→Run MemoryTree tooling without assuming the target repository already has a Python environment and without polluting the repository by default.
     8→
     9→## Preferred Order
    10→
    11→1. Use `uv run python ...` when `uv` is already available on the host machine.
    12→2. Otherwise use a working system `python`.
    13→3. If neither runner is available:
    14→   - for fresh init, fall back to direct template scaffolding from `assets/templates/`
    15→   - for upgrade or detection, fall back to conservative manual review and only make low-risk changes
    16→4. Ask the user before creating a project-local virtual environment, installing Python, or adding Python tooling files to the repository.
    17→
    18→## Why
    19→
    20→- MemoryTree is a repo-level memory workflow, not a language-specific build tool.
    21→- Many target repositories are not Python projects.
    22→- Auto-creating `.venv`, `pyproject.toml`, lockfiles, or other tooling files can pollute the host repository and conflict with its workflow.
    23→
    24→## Fresh Init
    25→
    26→For `scripts/init-memorytree.py` and first-run scaffolding:
    27→
    28→- Prefer running the script through `uv run python` or system `python`.
    29→- If no runner exists, create the initial files directly from `assets/templates/<locale>/`.
    30→- Do not block first-run installation only because Python is unavailable.
    31→
    32→## Upgrade And Detection
    33→
    34→For `scripts/upgrade-memorytree.py` and `scripts/detect-memorytree-locale.py`:
    35→
    36→- Prefer running the script through `uv run python` or system `python`.
    37→- If no runner exists, do not silently create a new environment inside the project.
    38→- Fall back to manual detection using the reference docs and keep the change set minimal.
    39→- If correct upgrade behavior depends on repo-specific policy interpretation, ask the user before proceeding.
    40→
    41→## Stop And Ask
    42→
    43→Stop and ask the user before environment setup when any of these are true:
    44→
    45→1. `uv` is unavailable and `python` is unavailable.
    46→2. Running the scripts would require creating a new `.venv` or installing host tooling.
    47→3. The repo has policies about local tooling, bootstrap scripts, or checked-in environment files.
    48→4. The upgrade is risky enough that manual fallback would be error-prone.
    49→

1→# AGENTS.md
     2→
     3→## Read Order
     4→- Read this file first.
     5→- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
     6→- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
     7→- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
     8→- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.
     9→- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.
    10→
    11→## Memory Rules
    12→- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
    13→- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
    14→- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
    15→- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
    16→- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.
    17→
    18→## Transcript Rules
    19→- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
    20→- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
    21→- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
    22→- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
    23→- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.
    24→- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.
    25→
    26→## Git Rules
    27→- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
    28→- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
    29→- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
    30→- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
    31→- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
    32→

1→# Upgrade Path
     2→
     3→Use this file when the target repository already has some project policy or partial MemoryTree files.
     4→
     5→## Goal
     6→
     7→Introduce MemoryTree without overwriting the host repository's existing rules.
     8→
     9→## When To Upgrade Instead Of Init
    10→
    11→Use the upgrade path when any of these are true:
    12→
    13→- The repo already has an `AGENTS.md`.
    14→- The repo already has a `Memory/` directory but is missing some required subfolders or active files.
    15→- The repo already has custom branch, PR, CI, or review policies that must remain the source of truth.
    16→
    17→## Safe Upgrade Rules
    18→
    19→1. Create missing `Memory/` directories.
    20→2. Create missing goal, todo, and chat-log files.
    21→3. Preserve an existing `AGENTS.md` by default.
    22→4. Report whether `AGENTS.md` still needs a manual merge to mention MemoryTree read order, active-file rules, isolated commit/PR flow, and repo-safety rules.
    23→5. Do not rewrite existing goals, todos, chat logs, or repo docs unless the user asked for it.
    24→
    25→## Script
    26→
    27→Use `scripts/upgrade-memorytree.py`.
    28→
    29→Recommended defaults:
    30→
    31→```text
    32→--root <repo> --locale auto --format json
    33→```
    34→
    35→## Expected Output
    36→
    37→The upgrade script should tell you:
    38→
    39→- what state it found
    40→- which files it created
    41→- which files it preserved
    42→- whether `AGENTS.md` needs a manual merge
    43→
    44→Current merge detection is intentionally conservative. An existing `AGENTS.md` is only treated as ready when it already covers these concepts, even if the wording differs:
    45→
    46→- MemoryTree read order
    47→- `Memory/01_goals`, `Memory/02_todos`, and `Memory/03_chat_logs`
    48→- active todo synchronization
    49→- append-only chat logs
    50→- a MemoryTree-scoped commit title such as `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>` or `docs(memorytree): <subject>`
    51→- a dedicated MemoryTree branch and PR flow
    52→- repo safety rules such as no direct-branch bypass and only enabling auto-merge when repo rules permit it
    53→

1→interface:
     2→  display_name: "MemoryTree"
     3→  short_description: "Repo memory scaffolding, deterministic transcript import and discovery, and isolated MemoryTree PR workflow"
     4→  default_prompt: "Use $memorytree-workflow to detect repo state, choose the right locale, maintain MemoryTree files, import or discover supported AI transcripts with deterministic code, keep current-project mirrors separate from the global archive, and keep MemoryTree-only commits isolated in their own branch and PR flow."
     5→

    13→_spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("upgrade_memorytree", str(SCRIPTS_DIR / "upgrade-memorytree.py"))
    14→_mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(_spec)
    15→_spec.loader.exec_module(_mod)

    30→FULL_EN_AGENTS = """\
    31→# AGENTS.md
    32→
    33→## Read Order
    34→- Read this file first.
    35→- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
    36→- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
    37→- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
    38→- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.
    39→
    40→## Memory Rules
    41→- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
    42→- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
    43→
    44→## Git Rules
    45→- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
    46→- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes.
    47→- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title: `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`.
    48→- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
    49→- If a diff includes product code, stop and ask the user.
    50→"""
    51→
    52→FULL_ZH_AGENTS = """\
    53→# AGENTS.md
    54→
    55→## 读取顺序
    56→- 先读本文件。

    58→- 读取与该目标绑定的 `Memory/02_todos/` 最新待办文件。
    59→- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/03_chat_logs/`。
    60→
    61→## 记忆规则
    62→- 当前待办必须与当前目标保持同步。
    63→- 对话日志只追加，禁止重写或删除既有内容。
    64→
    65→## Git 规则
    66→- 遵守当前仓库的分支、PR、CI、评审和发布规则。
    67→- 仅自动提交和推送 MemoryTree 自有变更。
    68→- 提交标题使用 memorytree(<scope>): <subject>。
    69→- 对 MemoryTree 专用变更使用专用分支和 PR；仅在仓库规则允许时才开启自动合并。
    70→"""
